heading: Emergency construction: fortifications
label: 7776 Emergency construction: fortifications
text: If in an emergency the President considers it urgent, a temporary fort or fortification may be built on private land if the owner consents in writing.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)477650:178., J. Res. 21, .Apr. 11, 189830 Stat. 737The word “important” is omitted as covered by the word “urgent”. The words “upon which such work is to be placed” are omitted as surplusage.
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 4776 of this title1970— struck out at end “In such a case, does not apply.”Pub. L. 91–393section 175 of title 50

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
